Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants an analysis of a provided event log representing a Procure to Pay P2P process I need to identify anomalous sequences explain why they are anomalous based on standard P2P best practices and detail the potential problems they cause

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Standard P2P Process What does a normal P2P flow look like Create Purchase Requisition PR Approve Purchase Requisition Issue Purchase Order PO Receive Goods Goods Receipt Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO 3 way match PO GR Invoice Release Payment Close Case Logical dependencies Approval must happen before PO issuance PO should ideally precede Goods Receipt Payment must follow the 3 way match Invoice matching needs PO GR and Invoice

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Analyze the Event Log Case by Case Case 1001 Assessment Perfect flow Standard sequential order No anomalies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Create PR Issue PO Wait no approval yet Vendor Invoice Received Goods not received yet Release Payment No match no goods receipt Approve Purchase Requisition Way too late happened after payment Receive Goods Happened after payment Match Invoice to PO Happened after payment Assessment Extremely anomalous Multiple severe control failures

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Release Payment No invoice no goods receipt yet Vendor Invoice Received Invoice arrived after payment Receive Goods Goods arrived after payment Match Invoice to PO Match happened after payment Assessment Highly anomalous Payment occurred before goods were received and before the invoice was even received or matched

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Create PR Approve PR Receive Goods Before PO was issued Issue Purchase Order Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case Assessment Anomalous Goods were received before the PO was formally issued

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize the Anomalies and Categorize Them Instead of just listing case by case it is better to group them by the type of business rule violation as this shows deeper process understanding

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly Type 1 Skipping the Approval Gate Segregation of Duties Authorization Failure Evidence Case 1002 issued the PO before the PR was approved Why problematic Bypasses spending limits budget controls and Segregation of Duties SoD Unauthorized purchases can be made

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly Type 2 Payment Prior to 3 Way Matching Fraud Overpayment Risk Evidence Case 1002 Payment before Match and GR Case 1003 Payment before Invoice Match and GR Why problematic The core control of P2P is the 3 way match PO equals GR equals Invoice Paying before this happens means the company might pay for goods never received pay the wrong amount or fall victim to duplicate billing fraud

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly Type 3 Receiving Goods Before Issuing a Purchase Order Blind Receiving Evidence Case 1004 Received Goods before Issued PO Why problematic Receiving blind means the warehouse does not know what was ordered in what quantity or at what price It leads to accepting unauthorized goods discrepancies in inventory and negotiating power is lost

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly Type 4 Out of Sequence Post Factual Processing Retroactive Document Handling Evidence Case 1002 Approval GR and Match all happened after payment Case 1003 Invoice and GR happened after payment Why problematic Indicates a process where documentation is falsified or backdated to justify an already executed payment High indicator of fraud or extreme process breakdown

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Draft the Response Introduction Briefly state what a standard P2P process looks like to set the baseline Requisition Approval PO GR Invoice 3 way match Payment Close Baseline Validation Note that Case 1001 represents the ideal compliant flow Detailed Anomaly Breakdown Use the categorized anomalies developed in Step 4 Reference specific Case IDs and timestamps to back up the claims Business Impact Risk Summary Group the why this is problematic into a strong concluding section focusing on Compliance Audit Financial Loss and Operational Inefficiency

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review and Refine Check against prompt Did I identify anomalies Yes Did I explain why they are problematic based on standard P2P practices Yes Tone Professional analytical audit oriented Clarity check Are the timestamps clearly referenced to prove the point Yes the logic holds up perfectly
